what_it_does: Defines the session-message resource contract: an immutable, append-only mailbox record identified by a cross-process-unique seq and an id mail-<seq>, carrying routing fields (to/from/verb/thread) and a content-addressed body (cas), and documents the inbox read operation session-message.list.
observations: Records are immutable once appended on an append-only mail leaf with keep-forever retention.
Identity uses plain seq-derived strings; there is deliberately no msg:// scheme yet (planned in CONTRACT-FORMAT §9.7).
The body is always behind a cas ref and is resolved to text by the inbox read.
thread defaults to the record's own id and is used to aggregate fan-out replies.
State model is stateless: no status field on records; consumption position is client-held or persisted via per-consumer cursor refs.
session-message.list is oldest-first, non-destructive, filterable by thread or verb, and paginated by since/next_since mail seq.
Verification entries show probe-verified runs dated 2026-06-12.
Read-side error session-message.bad-cursor covers corrupt or backward durable cursors; an empty inbox returns an honest empty list.
The file contains no code symbols.
standouts: Explicit rejection of read-modify-write on a shared log in favor of client-held cursors.
Separation of routable envelope (to/from/verb/thread) from content-addressed body (cas).
Planned msg:// scheme is intentionally deferred, leaving ids as plain strings.
conventions: Contract-entry frontmatter with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, and relates-to.
JSON schema embedded in a contract:schema fenced block.
Operation spec embedded in a contract:op fenced block with bindings, liveness, verification, and examples.
contract:error and contract:example blocks for error and example documentation.
Wiki-style resource links such as [[session]] and [[events]].
concerns: Status is building, so the contract is not stable.
HTTP binding is planned, not live.
additionalProperties: true in the schema allows arbitrary extra fields without validation.
Write semantics are not defined here; the only write paths referenced are session.post and supervisor writes.
notes: A contract spec for an immutable, cursor-driven mailbox read model with thread aggregation and content-addressed bodies.
questions: What are the exact semantics of the detail parameter in session-message.list?
How is the cas ref mechanically resolved to text during an inbox read?
What is the practical retention/expiration policy given keep-forever?
How do the write paths enforce that to/from/verb/cas are present before append?
When will the planned msg:// / thread:// schemes be introduced?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document the session-message resource and its list operation; the content matches, though it also delegates write semantics to session.post and adjacent resources.
apparent_themes: separation of routing envelope from body
intention_signals: Design a durable, audit-friendly inter-session messaging substrate where records are never mutated, consumers track their own read positions, and replies are grouped by thread for fan-out/consult patterns.
novelty: Deliberately avoids a msg:// URI scheme for message ids, using plain seq-derived strings as identity, and separates the routable envelope from the content-addressed body so the inbox read resolves text.
contribution: Provides a clear contract for an immutable mailbox record and a safe, non-destructive, cursor-based list operation with thread/verb filtering, which a fused system can reuse for audit-trail messaging semantics.
summary_for_embedding: Contract specification for session-message, an immutable append-only mailbox record with seq/id identity, routing envelope, content-addressed body, and the session-message.list inbox read operation.
intention_for_embedding: To standardize durable, cursor-driven, thread-aggregated inter-session messaging with non-destructive reads and content-addressed bodies.
